diseases.name: Rust
diseases.symptoms: Orange/brown pustules on leaves and stems
prevention: Spray Propiconazole or Mancozeb when symptoms appear

diseases.name: Powdery Mildew
diseases.symptoms: White powdery growth on leaves and stems
prevention: Spray wettable sulfur or recommended fungicide

pests.name: Aphids
pests.symptoms: Curling leaves, sticky honeydew, stunted growth
control: Use imidacloprid @ 0.5 ml/L if infestation is severe
prevention: Remove and destroy infested plant parts

pests.name: Army Worm
pests.symptoms: Leaf defoliation, caterpillars feeding on leaves at night
control: Spray chlorpyriphos @ 2.5 ml/L or cypermethrin @ 1 ml/L
Handpick and destroy caterpillars in small areas

pests.name: Wheat Stem Sawfly
pests.symptoms: Hollow stems, lodging of plants, white heads
control: Spray lambda-cyhalothrin @ 1 ml/L at stem elongation
Harvest at ground level to remove larvae
prevention: Stubble burning or deep plowing after harvest

diseases.name: Rice Blast
diseases.symptoms: Diamond-shaped lesions with gray center on leaves and neck

diseases.name: Bacterial Leaf Blight
diseases.symptoms: Yellowing and drying from leaf tip toward base
prevention: Use clean seed and resistant varieties
Avoid water movement from infected fields

pests.name: Brown Plant Hopper
pests.symptoms: Hopper burn, circular yellowing and drying of plants, honeydew secretion
control: Spray pymetrozine @ 0.6 g/L or imidacloprid @ 0.5 ml/L
Use neem oil @ 3 ml/L as organic option
Maintain 2-3 cm water depth during application
prevention: Monitor fields regularly with light traps

pests.name: Stem Borer
pests.symptoms: Dead hearts in vegetative stage, white ears in reproductive stage, bore holes in stem
control: Spray chlorantraniliprole @ 0.4 ml/L or cartap hydrochloride @ 1 g/L

pests.name: Leaf Folder
pests.symptoms: Leaves folded and fastened with silk, white and green striped appearance
control: Spray chlorpyriphos @ 2.5 ml/L or quinalphos @ 2 ml/L

diseases.name: Leaf Blight
diseases.symptoms: Brown, elongated lesions with yellow halos on leaves
prevention: Spray Mancozeb or similar fungicide if severe

diseases.name: Stem Rot
diseases.symptoms: Dark lesions on lower stems, lodging, wilting

pests.name: Fall Army Worm
pests.symptoms: Leaf defoliation, window-pane damage, frass near whorl
control: Spray emamectin benzoate @ 0.4 g/L or spinosad @ 0.5 ml/L
Use neem oil @ 5 ml/L for organic control
prevention: Early planting to avoid peak pest population
Conserve natural enemies like parasitic wasps

pests.name: Maize Stem Borer
pests.symptoms: Dead heart, shot holes in leaves, bore holes in stem
control: Spray carbaryl @ 2.5 g/L or chlorpyriphos @ 2.5 ml/L
Apply carbofuran granules in leaf whorl

pests.name: Corn Earworm
pests.symptoms: Feeding on silks and kernels, frass on ear tips
control: Spray emamectin benzoate @ 0.4 g/L at silking stage
Apply mineral oil drops on silks

diseases.name: Leaf Blotch
diseases.symptoms: Brown blotches on leaves

pests.name: Aphids
pests.symptoms: Curling leaves, sticky honeydew, stunted growth
control: Use imidacloprid @ 0.5 ml/L for severe infestation

pests.name: Army Worm
pests.symptoms: Leaf defoliation, caterpillars feeding in groups

pests.name: Barley Thrips
pests.symptoms: Silvery streaks on leaves, stunted growth

diseases.name: Downy Mildew
diseases.symptoms: Chlorotic streaks, downy growth on underside of leaves

pests.name: Shoot Fly
pests.symptoms: Dead heart, rotting of central shoot, fishy odor
control: Spray dimethoate @ 1 ml/L at 7 and 15 days after emergence
Use carbofuran granules in furrows at sowing

pests.name: Stem Borer
pests.symptoms: Dead hearts, bore holes in stem, red discoloration
control: Apply carbofuran granules in leaf whorl

pests.name: Aphids
pests.symptoms: Colonies on leaves, honeydew, sooty mold

diseases.symptoms: Systemic infection, pale leaves, malformed heads

pests.name: Pod Borer
pests.symptoms: Holes in pods, larvae feeding on seeds, webbed pods
control: Spray emamectin benzoate @ 0.4 g/L or indoxacarb @ 0.5 ml/L
Use neem oil @ 5 ml/L at pod formation
prevention: Early sowing to escape pest attack

pests.name: Aphids
pests.symptoms: Colonies on tender shoots and pods, sticky honeydew, sooty mold
control: Spray dimethoate @ 1 ml/L or imidacloprid @ 0.5 ml/L
Use neem oil @ 3 ml/L as organic option
prevention: Remove and destroy affected plant parts
Conserve natural enemies like ladybird beetles

pests.name: Cutworm
pests.symptoms: Young plants cut at ground level, wilting
control: Apply poison baits (rice bran + molasses + insecticide)
prevention: Handpick and destroy larvae at early stage

pests.name: Pod Borer

prevention: Intercropping with sorghum or pearl millet
